Treasure Island within the Golden Gate-Berkeley in foreground—San Francisco-Oakland Transbay Bridge on left
MEET SAN FRANCISCO
With Mary Hampton
I IS not merely the clanging little cable cars and the sidewalk stands of vivid flowers that makes
San Francisco different from any other city in the world. San Francisco is a city apart—with a per-
sonality as vivid and accidental as a scaramouche's. Like Topsy, it just growed, out of the strangest
mixture of gentle Mission fathers and Spanish adventurers, out of gentlemen from the old
south and madmen from across all the seas, rushing in with gun and shovel at the cry of gold. It
grew out of the keen-eyed shop-keepers and the rouged tarts, out of famous European chefs and
quick Chinese who came swarming at the heels of those who unearthed the gold. Suddenly, therefore, the
early little Yerba Buena outgrew its bounds. It began to push out over the hills—over all fourteen of them
finally—until the whole peninsula between bay and Pacific was covered with a new city. That is San Fran-
cisco today.
Many prospectors found themselves millionaires over night. So to them and their bags of gold came
trekking, quickly enough, comforts to be sold, and luxuries to be bartered for a bag of gold. It was then that
the world-and San Francisco—noticed that it happened to sit beside one of the three greatest, natural
harbors in the world. The rest just happened.
With their quick gold these hordes of rich adventurers early developed a fabulous standard of living-
all easy-come and easy-go. They spent their gold and shot their guns and drank their straight whiskey with
story-book abandon. And they loved as best they could, for women were extremely scarce. Perhaps for
this reason too, there was a sky-high premium on entertainment. Audiences poured out a personal apprecia-

MEET
SAN FRANCICO
MEET
SAN FRANCIS CO
Compliments of UNITED AIR LINES

GOING...
NORTH? EAST? SOUTH?
IT'S THRIFTY TO FLY UNITED
You save many hours of valuable time-and costs
are comparable with first class surface travel!
THEN you are ready to leave San
Francisco, remember this:
United Air Lines offers fast, fre-
quent transportation in any direc-
tion you want to go... north, south,
or east!
And the economy of United's lux-
urious air travel will surprise you.
For instance, suppose you're plan-
ning to visit the other great fair.
From here to New York, United's
fare is only $149.95-with 10% off
on round trips. And that includes
everything, even meals aloft. Tips
aren't accepted.
****
Going north, south or east, flying
in a United Mainliner is the quick-
est, most convenient way to travel.
In planning your trip-first call
DOuglas 1681 and find out how
little it will cost you to fly United!
Tickets and information: 400 Post St.,
San Francisco; telephone DOuglas 1681.
Or Travel Bureaus, Hotels, Telegraph Offices.
UNITED AIR LINES
The Main Line Airway
MORE THAN A ROUTE...A STANDARD OF SERVICE
